primary_sources.finding: AudioContext output timestamps expose context and performance clock positions for browser-side render correlation; they are not an audibility measurement.

DOMHighResTimeStamp values are interpreted relative to a defined time origin, so evidence components require one preserved session and explicit correlation rather than timestamp comparison across unrelated contexts.

finding: Browser audio evidence becomes materially stronger when clock samples, clock evaluation, route observations, diagnostic pulse identity, deployed commit, freshness, and optional audibility observation are bound into one digest-checked session packet while keeping processing and audibility as separate claim classes.
evidence_strength: tested contract; no live browser or device observation
source_status: primary standards plus repository-local prior artifacts
uncertainty: The packet cannot establish that a browser implementation reports timestamps correctly, that a device emitted acoustic energy, or that a human heard the signal.
falsification_route: Mutate any bound component and require digest rejection.
Compose components from different session identifiers and require rejection.
Supply stale evidence and require rejection.
Supply inconsistent or incomplete clock evidence and require rejection.
Compare a packet reporting processing without audibility against an external device-level measurement.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime with byte-identical pre-commit files
